element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Foundation of empire—players begin with one or two settlers to found initial cities
Core mechanic; players decide placement and improvements iteratively
Knowledge advancement system drives all progression; foundational for unlocking units and improvements
Land transformation for resource optimization; settlers perform these tasks
Persistent low-level threat that diminishes as exploration/settlement progresses
2-7 computer-controlled rivals with distinct traits; escalate competition over time

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Early game (as civilizations encounter each other)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Relationship management; alliances and warfare negotiation
Combat escalates as civilizations compete; access depends on tech advancement
Resource management; interconnected with city improvements and tax rates
Strategic choice affecting civilization behavior and growth
Late-game infrastructure evolution from roads; enables rapid logistical control
End-condition technologies; represent civilizational apex and potential victory conditions

typical_relief_triggers: Acquiring new resources via terrain improvements
typical_tension_triggers: Uncertainty of AI opponent behavior and intentions
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Long-cycle turn-based progression with escalating stakes; early game emphasizes exploration and settlement safety, mid-game introduces competition and conflict, late-game focuses on technological race and end-condition achievement

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Unexplored terrain and unclaimed huts contain barbarians and resources; exploration gradually removes threat sources
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Barbarian spawning tied to unmapped/unsettled regions; claiming territory through settlement and roads reduces threat emergence

difficulty_curve.early_game: Low to moderate; players manage settler expansion and barbarian harassment from huts in undiscovered areas; threat diminishes with exploration
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Moderate to high; multiple AI civilizations become active competitors; military units proliferate; technological race intensifies
difficulty_curve.late_game: High; competition for advanced technologies (flight, nuclear fission, spaceflight) creates existential stakes; potential for military superpowers and civilization collapse
notable_spikes: First contact with rival civilizations (diplomacy/warfare becomes viable)
Race toward space-age technologies (final victory condition)

notable_level_patterns.description: Players expand outward from starting settler(s), founding cities to claim resources and reduce barbarian spawn zones; spatial control reduces threats over time
notable_level_patterns.example_level: 4000 BC early game across Play on Earth or random world maps

notable_level_patterns.description: Access to units, improvements, and victory conditions locked behind sequential technology research; forces strategic prioritization of knowledge advancement
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Progression from Pottery/Wheel to Military units to Spaceflight

notable_level_patterns.description: 2-7 AI opponents with distinct civilization traits (e.g., Aztecs: expansionist and wealthy) create dynamic pressure; each civilization has own tech pace and diplomatic tendencies
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Any full game with multiple active rivals

notable_level_patterns.description: Decisions compound: city placement affects resources, which enables specific improvements, which unlock units, which enable military/exploration, which affects diplomacy with rivals
